evals.prompt: Can you prove that the sum of the first 1000 prime numbers is itself a prime number?
evals.expected_output: DISPROVED — the sum 3,682,913 is composite (13 × 29 × 9,769). Proof script uses sympy, passes all validation checks.
expectations: The proof correctly identifies the sum as 3,682,913
The proof finds that 3,682,913 is NOT prime and outputs DISPROVED
The proof provides a factorization as counterexample
A CLAIM_FORMAL dict with operator_note is present
The proof script is self-contained and runnable via python proof.py
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
proof.py includes FACT_REGISTRY with A-prefixed IDs
proof.py __main__ emits === PROOF SUMMARY (JSON) === block
JSON summary contains fact_registry (A-types with method/result), claim_formal, verdict, key_results
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
proof.md has executive summary with key numbers under verdict
proof.md uses inline fact references (A1, A2)
proof_audit.md contains Type A facts table, computation traces, and hardening checklist
proof_audit.md omits Type B sections (pure-math proof)

evals.prompt: Is it really true that the State of Israel is over 70 years old? Prove it rigorously.
evals.expected_output: PROVED (with unverified citations) — Israel founded May 14, 1948, age is 77 years as of March 2026, which is > 70.
expectations: Two independent sources are cited for the founding date
Dates are parsed from quotes using parse_date_from_quote, not hand-typed
Citations are verified by fetching URLs using verify_all_citations
date.today() is used for system time anchoring
CLAIM_FORMAL has operator '>' with operator_note explaining the choice
At least 3 adversarial checks are structurally independent
Cross-check confirms both sources agree on the founding date
Verdict is PROVED or PROVED (with unverified citations)
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
proof.py includes FACT_REGISTRY mapping B1, B2 to source keys
proof.py __main__ emits === PROOF SUMMARY (JSON) === block
JSON summary contains fact_registry, claim_formal, citations (with normalized status/method/coverage_pct), extractions, cross_checks, adversarial_checks, verdict, key_results
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
proof.md has executive summary with key numbers under verdict
proof.md uses inline fact references (B1, B2)
proof_audit.md contains full evidence table with quotes and URLs sourced from JSON summary citations
proof_audit.md citation details use normalized status/method fields from JSON (not parsed from message strings)
proof_audit.md contains CLAIM_FORMAL specification and FACT_REGISTRY from JSON summary
proof_audit.md contains computation traces reproduced from explain_calc inline output
proof_audit.md sections labeled with provenance
proof_audit.md hardening checklist is complete
proof.md conclusion states impact of any unverified citations

evals.prompt: Prove that Python is the best programming language
evals.expected_output: Skill correctly identifies this as an opinion/value judgment and declines to produce a proof. Offers factual alternatives.
expectations: No proof script is generated
The response identifies the claim as a value judgment or opinion
The response explains that 'best' has no objective definition without criteria
The response offers factual alternatives that could be proven

evals.prompt: Fact-check this: Earth's average temperature has risen by more than 1 degree Celsius since 1880.
evals.expected_output: PROVED or PROVED (with unverified citations) — multiple sources confirm ~1.1C warming. Proof uses multiple independent sources, some may have partial citation verification due to page formatting.
expectations: At least 3 independent empirical sources cited (NOAA, NASA, IPCC, etc.)
FACT_REGISTRY has B1, B2, B3+ entries, each mapping to a distinct source key
Each source is its own fact row in proof.md Evidence Summary
JSON summary citations object has entries for each B-prefixed fact ID with normalized status/method/coverage_pct
JSON summary extractions object has entries for each extracted value
proof_audit.md shows per-source citation verification using normalized status and method from JSON
Any Partially verified citation is clearly distinguished from Verified in proof_audit.md
proof_audit.md Citation Verification Details has impact field for non-verified citations labeled as author analysis
proof.md Proof Logic references fact IDs inline and notes source agreement where applicable
proof.md conclusion addresses any unverified citations with impact on specific conclusions
Fact IDs are consistent across proof.py JSON summary, proof.md, and proof_audit.md
proof_audit.md extraction records include method narrative labeled as author analysis
proof_audit.md computation traces reproduced verbatim from explain_calc inline output
Three output files: proof.py, proof.md, proof_audit.md
validate_proof.py passes with no issues
